#nullable enable

#pragma warning disable CS1570 // XML comment has badly formed XML

namespace OpenTelemetry.SemanticConventions;

/// <summary>
/// Constants for event names and typed payload structs outlined by the OpenTelemetry specifications.
/// </summary>
static partial class ExceptionEvents
{
    /// <summary>
    /// This event describes a single exception.
    ///
    /// </summary>
    public const string EventException = "exception";

    /// <summary>
    /// This event describes a single exception.
    ///
    /// </summary>
    public static partial class ExceptionDescriptor
    {
        public const string Name = "exception";
        public const string Brief = "This event describes a single exception.\n";
        public const string Note = "";
        public const string EmissionTarget = "unspecified";
        public const bool HasBody = false;
        public const string BodyJson = "";
        public const int AttributeCount = 4;
        public const int EntityAssociationCount = 0;
        public const string AttributeExceptionEscaped = "exception.escaped";
        public const string AttributeExceptionEscapedRequirementLevel = "recommended";
        public const string AttributeExceptionMessage = "exception.message";
        public const string AttributeExceptionMessageRequirementLevel = "conditionally_required";
        public const string AttributeExceptionMessageRequirementCondition = "Required if `exception.type` is not set, recommended otherwise.";
        public const string AttributeExceptionMessageNote = "> [!WARNING]\n>\n> This attribute may contain sensitive information.\n";
        public const int AttributeExceptionMessageExampleCount = 2;
        public const string AttributeExceptionMessageExample1 = "Division by zero";
        public const string AttributeExceptionMessageExample2 = "Can't convert 'int' object to str implicitly";
        public const string AttributeExceptionStacktrace = "exception.stacktrace";
        public const string AttributeExceptionStacktraceRequirementLevel = "recommended";
        public const string AttributeExceptionType = "exception.type";
        public const string AttributeExceptionTypeRequirementLevel = "conditionally_required";
        public const string AttributeExceptionTypeRequirementCondition = "Required if `exception.message` is not set, recommended otherwise.";
        public const string AttributeExceptionTypeNote = "If the recorded exception type is a wrapper that is not meaningful for\nfailure classification, instrumentation MAY use the type of the inner\nexception instead. For example, in Go, errors created with `fmt.Errorf`\nusing `%w` MAY be unwrapped when the wrapper type does not help\nclassify the failure.\n";
        public const int AttributeExceptionTypeExampleCount = 2;
        public const string AttributeExceptionTypeExample1 = "java.net.ConnectException";
        public const string AttributeExceptionTypeExample2 = "OSError";
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// This event describes a single exception.
    ///
    /// </summary>
    public readonly record struct ExceptionPayload
    {
        /// <summary>
        /// Indicates that the exception is escaping the scope of the span.
        ///
        /// </summary>
        /// <remarks>
        /// Requirement level: recommended.
        /// </remarks>
        [global::System.Obsolete("Removed, no replacement.")]
        public bool? ExceptionEscaped { get; init; }

        /// <summary>
        /// The exception message.
        /// </summary>
        /// <remarks>
        /// Requirement level: conditionally_required.
        /// Required if `exception.type` is not set, recommended otherwise.
        /// > [!WARNING]
        /// >
        /// > This attribute may contain sensitive information.
        ///
        /// Examples:
        /// - Division by zero
        /// - Can't convert 'int' object to str implicitly
        /// </remarks>
        public string? ExceptionMessage { get; init; }

        /// <summary>
        /// A stacktrace as a string in the natural representation for the language runtime. The representation is to be determined and documented by each language SIG.
        ///
        /// </summary>
        /// <remarks>
        /// Requirement level: recommended.
        /// </remarks>
        public string? ExceptionStacktrace { get; init; }

        /// <summary>
        /// The type of the exception (its fully-qualified class name, if applicable). The dynamic type of the exception should be preferred over the static type in languages that support it.
        ///
        /// </summary>
        /// <remarks>
        /// Requirement level: conditionally_required.
        /// Required if `exception.message` is not set, recommended otherwise.
        /// If the recorded exception type is a wrapper that is not meaningful for
        /// failure classification, instrumentation MAY use the type of the inner
        /// exception instead. For example, in Go, errors created with `fmt.Errorf`
        /// using `%w` MAY be unwrapped when the wrapper type does not help
        /// classify the failure.
        ///
        /// Examples:
        /// - java.net.ConnectException
        /// - OSError
        /// </remarks>
        public string? ExceptionType { get; init; }
    }
}
